ops.title: Editing a cc-guides doc (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md) means editing its .claude/fragments/<artifact>/*.fragment.md and running `cc-guides render`; the brew binary (0.1.21) is now on PATH and passes the Guides CI check, but render also bumps cc-guides.lock — reve

ops.body:
Repos scaffolded with a `guides.yml` workflow (cc-notes and siblings) render AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/`.claude/settings.json` from `.claude/fragments/<ARTIFACT>/*.fragment.md` via cc-guides; a push-time Guides job re-renders and byte-compares. `AGENTS.md` is NOT hand-editable (header: `<!-- GENERATED by cc-guides from .claude/fragments/AGENTS.md/ — do not edit; edit the fragments and run 'cc-guides render'. -->`) — edit the fragment, then render.

As of 2026-07-14 the older notes here are stale: **`cc-guides` is now brew-installed and on PATH** (`/opt/homebrew/bin/cc-guides`, 0.1.21), and the GENERATED header **no longer carries a version stamp** (just the fragments path). Rendering AGENTS.md with brew 0.1.21 produced a clean diff and the **Guides CI check passed** (`yasyf/cc-guides@action-v1`) — so the local brew binary is compatible with CI; no release-binary download dance was needed this time.

**Why / the gotcha:** `cc-guides render` regenerates ALL artifacts and **re-resolves the `@main` sources it pins, bumping `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock`** (e.g. the `cc-skills` commit) as a side effect. That lock bump is an unrelated dependency change and does NOT affect AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md content (they render from local fragments).

**How to apply:** edit the `*.fragment.md`, run `env -u GOROOT cc-guides render`, then `jj restore .claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock` to drop the incidental cc-skills bump. Verify with `jj diff AGENTS.md` that the diff is ONLY your intended lines (no stamp/reflow churn) — a clean diff means the local binary matches CI; commit fragment + rendered artifact together. If a future render DOES churn a version stamp, fall back to the CI-pinned release binary. Related: [[cc-skills-marketplace-skill-sources]].
